description: Tonight on 10’s Late News – Fugitive cop killer Dezi Freeman has been shot dead during a stand-off with police in Victoria’s north-east, petrol prices are expected to drop by more than 26 cents a litre from Wednesday after the federal government slashed the fuel excise, Iran has issued a chilling warning after US President Donald Trump said he is considering seizing Kharg Island, there’s fears shipping disruptions could get worse as the Iranian-backed Houthis enter the Middle East conflict, Melbourne Storm forward Tui Kamikamica is in hospital after a suspected stroke, a massage parlour on the Gold Coast has been destroyed in an early morning blaze, the US has allowed an oil tanker to deliver crucial energy supplies to Cuba, a fire has torn through a plastic factory in Indonesia, and Cadbury World has created a giant easter egg weighing 55 kilograms. title: NASA Set To Launch Artemis II Mission For First Crewed Moon Return In 53 Years | 10 News
description: NASA has started the countdown to launch the first crew of astronauts toward the moon in over 53 years with its second Artemis mission taking them deeper into space than humans have ever gone before.  One Canadian and three American astronauts are due for liftoff aboard NASA's Orion capsule and Space Launch System rocket on Wednesday for a 10-day test mission swinging around the moon and back.  NASA officials said the Artemis II mission remains on track, with no major issues ahead of launch at a press briefing on Monday in Florida’s Cape Canaveral.
